blurb: UK technology trade association with regular AI-policy analysis. Launch coverage uses first-party EU/UK AI regulation briefings.

hiring.note: Official techUK careers page verified; readable page exposed only register-interest/experienced/early-careers links, no current policy role to add.

publications.title: EU releases final version of AI Code of Practice
publications.summary: techUK summarises the EU GPAI Code of Practice and says it will continue analysing how to support members under the Code.

publications.title: UK Government puts forward plans to regulate AI
publications.summary: techUK briefs members on the UK Government response to the AI White Paper consultation and the pro-innovation/context-based regulatory approach.

publications.title: UK industries unite to accelerate AI adoption
publications.summary: techUK announces an AI Adoption Coalition across Industrial Strategy sectors to share responsible-adoption practice, surface common barriers and engage government on delivery of sectoral AI Adoption Plans.

publications.title: If generative AI changed how we produce content, agentic AI will change how we work
publications.summary: A techUK-hosted City of London policy essay argues that responsible agentic-AI deployment depends on governance, auditability, workforce capability and clear accountability, especially in regulated financial and professional services.

publications.title: Agentic AI needs privacy-preserving infrastructure for real-world success
publications.summary: techUK-hosted industry view on agentic-AI governance argues that privacy-preserving infrastructure, federated learning and strong controls are needed for production deployment in sensitive sectors.

publications.title: Agentic AI: The Shift from Task Automation to Service Orchestration
publications.summary: techUK-hosted industry view frames agentic AI as a public-sector and regulated-services governance problem, stressing identity, data controls, observability, accountability and human intervention.

publications.title: The release of the international AI safety report 2026: navigating rapid AI advancement and emerging risks
publications.summary: techUK summarises the International AI Safety Report 2026, highlighting frontier capability advances, bio/cyber misuse risks and governance challenges ahead of India’s AI Impact Summit.

publications.title: DSIT's AI Assurance Roadmap Takes its Next Step: techUK to Lead the Industry Advisory Group of AI Assurance Consortium
publications.summary: techUK to lead the Industry Advisory Group of the new DSIT-backed, BCS-chaired AI Assurance Consortium — a direct AI assurance/governance role.

publications.title: DCMS published its long-awaited Media Green Paper
publications.summary: Summarises the DCMS Media Green Paper and flags its open consultation (responses due 31 Aug 2026).

publications.title: Northern Growth Strategy: call for consultation responses
publications.summary: techUK call for member input into the Government's North of England Growth Strategy consultation.

notes: launch_harvest 2026-06-27: direct fetch HTTP 200 on EU Code first-party resource. followup_harvest 2026-06-28: direct techUK/GOV.UK fetches verified 2026-06 recent AI-adoption and agentic-AI governance items; suggested newsletters treated as discovery only. followup_harvest 2026-06-28: added three more verified June 2026 techUK items (AI Assurance Consortium role, DCMS Media Green Paper consultation, Northern Growth Strategy call).

news.title: Government to partner with tech companies, trade unions and industry leaders to boost AI adoption and equip workers with AI skills
news.summary: GOV.UK names techUK as convening UK trade associations for an AI Adoption Coalition alongside the government’s AI Adoption Summit package and sectoral AI Adoption Plans.
